This Act is binding on Her Majesty in right of Canada, except in matters respecting the Yukon Government or the Government of the Northwest Territories or Nunavut.
[Repealed, 2002, c. 7, s. 128]
[Repealed, 2014, c. 2, s. 11]

records.heading: Idem
records.text: The exception referred to in subsection (1) shall come into operation in respect of the Government of Nunavut on a day to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.
[Repealed, 2008, c. 30, s. 1]
It is a discriminatory practice for an employee organization on a prohibited ground of discrimination
to exclude an individual from full membership in the organization
to expel or suspend a member of the organization; or

to limit, segregate, classify or otherwise act in relation to an individual in a way that would deprive the individual of employment opportunities, or limit employment opportunities or otherwise adversely affect the status of the individual, where the individual is a member of the organization or where any of the obligations of the organization pursuant to a collective agreement relate to the individual.
[Repealed, 1998, c. 9, s. 12]

records.heading: Transitional
records.text: Every Tribunal appointed prior to the coming into force of this Act shall continue to act as though this Part had not come into force.
commencement day In this section,means the day on which this section comes into force.

records.heading: Members cease to hold office
records.text: Subject to subsections (3), (4) and (5), the members of the Human Rights Tribunal Panel cease to hold office on the commencement day.

records.heading: Continuing jurisdiction of Human Rights Tribunal
records.text: Canadian Human Rights Act The members of any Human Rights Tribunal appointed under thebefore the commencement day have jurisdiction with respect to any inquiry into the complaint in respect of which the Human Rights Tribunal was appointed.

records.heading: Continuing jurisdiction of Review Tribunal
records.text: Canadian Human Rights Act The members of any Review Tribunal constituted under thebefore the commencement day have jurisdiction with respect to any appeal against a decision or order of a Human Rights Tribunal.

records.heading: Continuing jurisdiction of Employment Equity Review Tribunal
records.text: Employment Equity Act The members of any Employment Equity Review Tribunal established under section 28 or 39 of thebefore the commencement day have jurisdiction over any matter in respect of which the Tribunal was established.

records.heading: Supervision by Chairperson of Canadian Human Rights Tribunal
records.text: The Chairperson of the Canadian Human Rights Tribunal has supervision over and direction of the work of any Human Rights Tribunal, Review Tribunal or Employment Equity Review Tribunal referred to in subsection (3), (4) or (5).

records.heading: Remuneration
records.text: Each member of a Human Rights Tribunal, Review Tribunal or Employment Equity Review Tribunal referred to in subsection (3), (4) or (5), other than such a member who is appointed as a full-time member of the Canadian Human Rights Tribunal, shall be paid such remuneration as may be fixed by the Governor in Council.

records.heading: Travel expenses
records.text: Each member of a Human Rights Tribunal, Review Tribunal or Employment Equity Review Tribunal referred to in subsection (3), (4) or (5) is entitled to be paid travel and living expenses incurred in carrying out duties as a member of that Tribunal while absent from their place of residence, but the expenses must not exceed the maximum limits authorized by Treasury Board directive for employees of the Government of Canada.

records.heading: Pardons in effect — references in other legislation
records.text: Criminal Records Act A reference to a record suspension in the following provisions, as enacted by this Part, is deemed also to be a reference to a pardon that is granted or issued under the:
conviction for an offence for which a pardon has been granted or in respect of which a record suspension has been ordered Canadian Human Rights Act the definitionin section 25 of the

records.heading: Canadian Human Rights Act Complaints —
records.text: Canadian Human Rights Act Budget Implementation Act, 2009 431 The, as it read immediately before the day on which this section comes into force, applies with respect to any complaint filed before that day under section 40 of that Act, other than a complaint referred to in subsection 396(1) of the, as amended by subsection(1) of this Act.

This Act does not affect the status of an employee who, immediately before the coming into force of this subsection, occupied a position in the Canadian Human Rights Commission and performed services on a full-time basis for the Human Rights Tribunal Panel, except that the employee shall, on the coming into force of this subsection, occupy that position in the Canadian Human Rights Tribunal.

records.heading: employee Definition of
records.text: employee Public Service Employment Act In this section,has the same meaning as in subsection 2(1) of the.

records.heading: Regard to legal traditions and customary laws
records.text: Canadian Human Rights Act Indian Act In relation to a complaint made under theagainst a First Nation government, including a band council, tribal council or governing authority operating or administering programs and services under the, this Act shall be interpreted and applied in a manner that gives due regard to First Nations legal traditions and customary laws, particularly the balancing of individual rights and interests against collective rights and interests, to the extent that they are consistent with the principle of gender equality.

Canadian Human Rights Act Within five years after the day on which this Act receives royal assent, a comprehensive review of the effects of the repeal of section 67 of theshall be jointly undertaken by the Government of Canada and any organizations identified by the Minister of Indian Affairs and Northern Development as being, in the aggregate, representative of the interests of First Nations peoples throughout Canada.

records.heading: Report
records.text: A report on the review referred to in subsection (1) shall be submitted to both Houses of Parliament within one year after the day on which the review is undertaken under that subsection.

records.heading: Grace period
records.text: Indian Act Canadian Human Rights Act Despite section 1, an act or omission by any First Nation government, including a band council, tribal council or governing authority operating or administering programs or services under the, that was made in the exercise of powers or the performance of duties and functions conferred or imposed by or under that Act shall not constitute the basis for a complaint under Part III of theif it occurs within 36 months after the day on which this Act receives royal assent.

records.heading: Study to be undertaken
records.text: Canadian Human Rights Act The Government of Canada, together with the appropriate organizations representing the First Nations peoples of Canada, shall, within the period referred to in section 3, undertake a study to identify the extent of the preparation, capacity and fiscal and human resources that will be required in order for First Nations communities and organizations to comply with the. The Government of Canada shall report to both Houses of Parliament on the findings of that study before the expiration of the period referred to in section 3.

records.heading: Interpretation
records.text: 396 397 Public Sector Equitable Compensation Act Unless the context otherwise requires, words and expressions used in sectionsandhave the same meaning as in the.

425 Budget Implementation Act, 2018, No. 2 Canadian Human Rights Act The following complaints with respect to employees that are before the Canadian Human Rights Commission on the day on which this Act receives royal assent, or that are filed with that Commission during the period beginning on that day and ending on the day on which subsection(1) of thecomes into force, shall, despite section 44 of the, without delay, be referred by the Commission to the Board:

Canadian Human Rights Act complaints based on section 7 or 10 of the, if the complaint is in respect of the employer establishing or maintaining differences in wages between male and female employees; and
Canadian Human Rights Act complaints based on section 11 of the.

records.heading: Application of this section
records.text: The complaints referred to in subsection (1) shall be dealt with by the Board as required by this section.

records.heading: Powers of Board
records.text: Federal Public Sector Labour Relations Act Canadian Human Rights Act Equal Wages Guidelines, 1986 Budget Implementation Act 2018, No. 2 425 The Board has, in relation to a complaint referred to it, in addition to the powers conferred on it under the, the power to interpret and apply sections 7, 10 and 11 of the, and the, in respect of employees, even after the coming into force of subsection(1) of the.

records.heading: Summary examination
records.text: The Board shall review the complaint in a summary way and shall refer it to the employer that is the subject of the complaint, or to the employer that is the subject of the complaint and the bargaining agent of the employees who filed the complaint, as the Board considers appropriate, unless it appears to the Board that the complaint is trivial, frivolous or vexatious or was made in bad faith.

records.heading: Power to assist
records.text: If the Board refers a complaint under subsection (4) to an employer, or to an employer and a bargaining agent, it may assist them in resolving any matters relating to the complaint by any means that it considers appropriate.

records.heading: Hearing
records.text: If the employer, or the employer and the bargaining agent, as the case may be, do not resolve the matters relating to the complaint within 180 days after the complaint is referred to them, or any longer period or periods that may be authorized by the Board, the Board shall schedule a hearing.

records.heading: Procedure
records.text: The Board shall determine its own procedure but shall give full opportunity to the employer, or the employer and the bargaining agent, as the case may be, to present evidence and make submissions to it.

records.heading: Decision
records.text: The Board shall make a decision in writing in respect of the complaint and send a copy of its decision with the reasons for it to the employer, or the employer and the bargaining agent, as the case may be.

records.heading: Restriction
records.text: Canadian Human Rights Act Budget Implementation Act, 2018, No. 2 425 The Board has, in relation to complaints referred to in this section, the power to make any order that a member or panel may make under section 53 of the, except that no monetary remedy may be granted by the Board in respect of the complaint other than a lump sum payment, and the payment may be only in respect of a period that ends on or before the day on which subsection(1) of thecomes into force.

Subject to subsections (2) and (3), the Canadian Human Rights Tribunal shall inquire into the following complaints with respect to employees that are before it on the day on which this Act receives royal assent:

Canadian Human Rights Act complaints based on section 7 or 10 of the, if the complaint is in respect of the employer establishing or maintaining differences in wages between male and female employees; and
Canadian Human Rights Act complaints based on section 11 of the.

records.heading: Powers of Tribunal
records.text: 399 If sectionis in force when the Canadian Human Rights Tribunal inquires into a complaint referred to in subsection (1),
Canadian Human Rights Act complaints referred to in paragraph (1)(a) shall be dealt with as if sections 7 and 10 of thestill applied to those employees; and
Canadian Human Rights Act Equal Wage Guidelines, 1986 complaints referred to in paragraph (1)(b) shall be dealt with as if section 11 of theand thestill applied to those employees.

records.heading: Limitation
records.text: 394 No monetary remedy may be granted by the Canadian Human Rights Tribunal in respect of a complaint referred to in subsection (1) other than a lump sum payment, and the payment may only be in respect of a period that ends on or before the day on which sectioncomes into force.
Section 395 of the Act is repealed.
Section 396 of the Act is repealed.
Sections 397 to 399 of the Act are repealed.

Pooled Registered Pension Plans Act the terms of any pooled registered pension plan provide for variable payments, variable life payments or the transfer of funds only at a fixed age under sections 48, 51.1 and 55, respectively, of the
